new_technique_found.name: BSCE-GRA: Brier Score Calibration via Gradient Rescaling for XGBoost/LightGBM/CatBoost
new_technique_found.key_insight: Use the per-sample Brier score as a gradient re-weighting factor inside gradient boosting training. Samples where the model is most uncertain/miscalibrated receive amplified gradient updates: grad_i = (p_i - y_i) * (1 + lambda * (p_i - y_i)^2). Hessian scaled identically. This is different from focal loss (which uses single logit) because Brier uses the full probability mass (p and 1-p both contribute), giving more accurate uncertainty measurement. CVPR 2025. Code available.
new_technique_found.novelty_vs_pipeline: None of P001-P008 modify the gradient objective of the tree models on HF islands. P001 targets TabICL (neural), P006 is post-hoc MCBoost, P007 is monotone constraint (not loss). BSCE-GRA is the first proposal that changes HOW gradients are computed inside XGB/LGBM/CatBoost during training.
new_technique_found.implementation_complexity: easy (3 hours, hf-space/app.py custom objective function)
new_technique_found.fleet_coverage: 5 of 6 islands (all XGB/LGBM/CatBoost; S12 ExtraTrees excluded)

config_mutation.rationale: CVPR 2025 papers (March 2025) are < 1 year old and may have fewer citations despite high quality. Lowering citation floor from 5 to 0 for CVPR/NeurIPS/ICML-accepted papers surfaced BSCE-GRA (arXiv:2503.22725) which would have been filtered otherwise.
config_mutation.result: Found 1 novel technique at citation count 0 (very recent CVPR 2025). Consider keeping citation floor at 0 for accepted-venue papers.
